content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Delbert Spurlock, Chief
    Conflicts of Interest Division

Date : September 10, 197[6]

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION - Ken Goshorn

Subject: Telephone Conversation with Tom MacBride of the Public
         Utilities Commission, September 8, 1976


         Tom MacBride of the Public Utilities Commission called on
September 8, 1976, with an emergency problem.  Two Commission
employees (engineers) are going to Alaska to inspect gas fields,
etc.  They will later participate in decisions materially affecting the utilities holding interests in these sites.  PUC pays the
engineers' airfare from San Francisco to Alaska and their per
diem, etc.  However, while in Alaska they will fly on company
planes to inspect certain areas.  There is no feasible alternative
means of travel.

         I orally advised Mr. MacBride that this benefit, or "income",
so long as it was totally work-related, appeared to accrue to the
PUC, not the officials themselves.  The flights are in the nature
of a "plant tour" by the officials and their value should not be
construed as "income" to the individuals.

         We have made a record of this conversation in case there are
any repercussions later.

cc:   Tom MacBride
      Public Utilities Commission


bcc:  DHL
      BS
      KF

sections.question_synthetic: Whether PUC engineers receiving flights on company planes while inspecting gas fields in Alaska constitutes taxable income or a conflict of interest when they will later participate in decisions affecting those utilities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advised orally that company-provided flights constitute work-related benefits accruing to the PUC rather than personal income to the officials, and should not be construed as income to the individuals, analogous to a plant tour.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal FPPC memorandum documenting an oral advice call rather than a formal advice letter; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; oral advice was given on September 8, 1976 and memorialized on September 10, 1976; document lacks typical structured advice letter format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether PUC engineers receiving flights on company planes while inspecting gas fields in Alaska constitutes taxable income or a conflict of interest when they will later participate in decisions affecting those utilities.

CONCLUSION: FPPC advised orally that company-provided flights constitute work-related benefits accruing to the PUC rather than personal income to the officials, and should not be construed as income to the individuals, analogous to a plant tour.

Two Commission employees (engineers) are going to Alaska to inspect gas fields, etc. They will later participate in decisions materially affecting the utilities holding interests in these sites. PUC pays the engineers' airfare from San Francisco to Alaska and their per diem, etc. However, while in Alaska they will fly on company planes to inspect certain areas. There is no feasible alternative means of travel. I orally advised Mr. MacBride that this benefit, or "income", so long as it was totally work-related, appeared to accrue to the PUC, not the officials themselves. The flights are in the nature of a "plant tour" by the officials and their value should not be construed as "income" to the individuals. We have made a record of this conversation in case there are any repercussions later. cc: Tom MacBride Public Utilities Commission bcc: DHL BS KF
embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum documenting an oral advice given to the Public Utilities Commission regarding whether company-provided flights for engineers inspecting gas fields constitute personal income or conflict of interest.
